# ============================================================================
# EXTERNAL_POSITION_ESTIMATE (MAV_CMD 43003)
# Provide an external position estimate for use when dead-reckoning. This is
# meant to be used for occasional position resets that may be provided by a
# external system such as a remote pilot using landmarks over a video link.
# ============================================================================
COMMAND <%= target_name %> EXTERNAL_POSITION_ESTIMATE BIG_ENDIAN "Provide an external position estimate for use when dead-reckoning. This is meant to be used for occasional position resets that may be provided by a external system such as a remote pilot using landmarks over a video link."
  ACCESSOR JsonAccessor
  TEMPLATE '{"MSGNAME":"COMMAND_LONG","TARGET_SYSTEM":1,"TARGET_COMPONENT":1,"COMMAND":43003,"CONFIRMATION":0,"TRANSMISSION_TIME_1":0.0,"PROCESSING_TIME_2":0.0,"ACCURACY_3":0.0,"PARAM4":0.0,"LATITUDE_5":0.0,"LONGITUDE_6":0.0,"ALTITUDE_7":0.0}'
  # Sent as JSON - converted to MAVLink COMMAND_LONG by protocol

  APPEND_PARAMETER MSGNAME 0 STRING "COMMAND_LONG" "Message type"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TARGET_SYSTEM 8 UINT 0 255 1 "Target system ID"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TARGET_COMPONENT 8 UINT 0 255 1 "Target component ID"

  APPEND_PARAMETER COMMAND 16 UINT 43003 43003 43003 "MAV_CMD_EXTERNAL_POSITION_ESTIMATE"

  APPEND_PARAMETER CONFIRMATION 8 UINT 0 255 0 "0=First transmission, increment for retries"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TRANSMISSION_TIME_1 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "Timestamp that this message was sent as a time in the transmitters time domain. The sender should wrap this time back to zero based on required timing accuracy for the application and the limitations of a 32 bit float. For example, wrapping at 10 hours would give approximately 1ms accuracy. Recipient must handle time wrap in any timing jitter correction applied to this field. Wrap rollover time should not be at not more than 250 seconds, which would give approximately 10 microsecond accuracy."
    UNITS "Seconds" s

  APPEND_PARAMETER PROCESSING_TIME_2 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "The time spent in processing the sensor data that is the basis for this position. The recipient can use this to improve time alignment of the data. Set to zero if not known."
    UNITS "Seconds" s

  APPEND_PARAMETER ACCURACY_3 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "estimated one standard deviation accuracy of the measurement. Set to NaN if not known."

  APPEND_PARAMETER PARAM4 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "Empty"
    HIDDEN

  APPEND_PARAMETER LATITUDE_5 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "Latitude"

  APPEND_PARAMETER LONGITUDE_6 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "Longitude"

  APPEND_PARAMETER ALTITUDE_7 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "Altitude, not used. Should be sent as NaN. May be supported in a future version of this message."
    UNITS "Meters" m

  RESPONSE <%= target_name %> COMMAND_ACK

# ============================================================================
# MISSION PROTOCOL COMMANDS
# Native MAVLink messages (not COMMAND_LONG wrappers) used for uploading
# missions to the vehicle via the MAVLink mission protocol.
# See: https://mavlink.io/en/services/mission.html
# ============================================================================

# ============================================================================
# MISSION_CLEAR_ALL (ID 45)
# Delete all mission items at once.
# ============================================================================
COMMAND <%= target_name %> MISSION_CLEAR_ALL BIG_ENDIAN "Delete all mission items at once."
  ACCESSOR JsonAccessor
  TEMPLATE '{"MSGNAME":"MISSION_CLEAR_ALL","TARGET_SYSTEM":1,"TARGET_COMPONENT":1,"MISSION_TYPE":0}'
  # Native MAVLink message - encoded by protocol

  APPEND_PARAMETER MSGNAME 0 STRING "MISSION_CLEAR_ALL" "Message type"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TARGET_SYSTEM 8 UINT 0 255 1 "System ID"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TARGET_COMPONENT 8 UINT 0 255 1 "Component ID"

  # MAVLink 2 Extension Field
  APPEND_PARAMETER MISSION_TYPE 8 UINT 0 255 0 "Mission type."
    STATE MISSION 0
    STATE FENCE 1
    STATE RALLY 2
    STATE ALL 255


# ============================================================================
# MISSION_COUNT (ID 44)
# This message is emitted as response to MISSION_REQUEST_LIST by the MAV and
# to initiate a write transaction. The GCS can then request the individual
# mission item based on the knowledge of the total number of waypoints.
# ============================================================================
COMMAND <%= target_name %> MISSION_COUNT BIG_ENDIAN "This message is emitted as response to MISSION_REQUEST_LIST by the MAV and to initiate a write transaction. The GCS can then request the individual mission item based on the knowledge of the total number of waypoints."
  ACCESSOR JsonAccessor
  TEMPLATE '{"MSGNAME":"MISSION_COUNT","TARGET_SYSTEM":1,"TARGET_COMPONENT":1,"COUNT":0,"MISSION_TYPE":0,"OPAQUE_ID":0}'
  # Native MAVLink message - encoded by protocol

  APPEND_PARAMETER MSGNAME 0 STRING "MISSION_COUNT" "Message type"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TARGET_SYSTEM 8 UINT 0 255 1 "System ID"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TARGET_COMPONENT 8 UINT 0 255 1 "Component ID"

  APPEND_PARAMETER COUNT 16 UINT 0 65535 0 "Number of mission items in the sequence"

  # MAVLink 2 Extension Field
  APPEND_PARAMETER MISSION_TYPE 8 UINT 0 255 0 "Mission type."
    STATE MISSION 0
    STATE FENCE 1
    STATE RALLY 2
    STATE ALL 255

  APPEND_PARAMETER OPAQUE_ID 32 UINT 0 4294967295 0 "Id of current on-vehicle mission, fence, or rally point plan (on download from vehicle). This field is used when downloading a plan from a vehicle to a GCS. 0 on upload to the vehicle from GCS. 0 if plan ids are not supported. The current on-vehicle plan ids are streamed in `MISSION_CURRENT`, allowing a GCS to determine if any part of the plan has changed and needs to be re-uploaded. The ids are recalculated by the vehicle when any part of the on-vehicle plan changes (when a new plan is uploaded, the vehicle returns the new id to the GCS in MISSION_ACK)."


# ============================================================================
# MISSION_ITEM_INT (ID 73)
# Message encoding a mission item. This message is emitted to announce the
# presence of a mission item and to set a mission item on the system. The
# mission item can be either in x, y, z meters (type: LOCAL) or x:lat,
# y:lon, z:altitude. Local frame is Z-down, right handed (NED), global frame
# is Z-up, right handed (ENU). NaN or INT32_MAX may be used in float/integer
# params (respectively) to indicate optional/default values (e.g. to use the
# component's current latitude, yaw rather than a specific value). See also
# https://mavlink.io/en/services/mission.html.
# ============================================================================
COMMAND <%= target_name %> MISSION_ITEM_INT BIG_ENDIAN "Message encoding a mission item. This message is emitted to announce the presence of a mission item and to set a mission item on the system. The mission item can be either in x, y, z meters (type: LOCAL) or x:lat, y:lon, z:altitude. Local frame is Z-down, right handed (NED), global frame is Z-up, right handed (ENU). NaN or INT32_MAX may be used in float/integer params (respectively) to indicate optional/default values (e.g. to use the component's current latitude, yaw rather than a specific value). See also https://mavlink.io/en/services/mission.html."
  ACCESSOR JsonAccessor
  TEMPLATE '{"MSGNAME":"MISSION_ITEM_INT","TARGET_SYSTEM":1,"TARGET_COMPONENT":1,"SEQ":0,"FRAME":3,"COMMAND":0,"CURRENT":0,"AUTOCONTINUE":1,"PARAM1":0.0,"PARAM2":0.0,"PARAM3":0.0,"PARAM4":0.0,"X":0,"Y":0,"Z":0.0,"MISSION_TYPE":0}'
  # Native MAVLink message - encoded by protocol

  APPEND_PARAMETER MSGNAME 0 STRING "MISSION_ITEM_INT" "Message type"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TARGET_SYSTEM 8 UINT 0 255 1 "System ID"

  APPEND_PARAMETER TARGET_COMPONENT 8 UINT 0 255 1 "Component ID"

  APPEND_PARAMETER SEQ 16 UINT 0 65535 0 "Waypoint ID (sequence number). Starts at zero. Increases monotonically for each waypoint, no gaps in the sequence (0,1,2,3,4)."

  APPEND_PARAMETER FRAME 8 UINT 0 255 3 "The coordinate system of the waypoint."
    STATE GLOBAL 0
    STATE LOCAL_NED 1
    STATE MISSION 2
    STATE GLOBAL_RELATIVE_ALT 3
    STATE LOCAL_ENU 4
    STATE GLOBAL_INT 5
    STATE GLOBAL_RELATIVE_ALT_INT 6
    STATE LOCAL_OFFSET_NED 7
    STATE BODY_NED 8
    STATE BODY_OFFSET_NED 9
    STATE GLOBAL_TERRAIN_ALT 10
    STATE GLOBAL_TERRAIN_ALT_INT 11
    STATE BODY_FRD 12
    STATE RESERVED_13 13
    STATE RESERVED_14 14
    STATE RESERVED_15 15
    STATE RESERVED_16 16
    STATE RESERVED_17 17
    STATE RESERVED_18 18
    STATE RESERVED_19 19
    STATE LOCAL_FRD 20
    STATE LOCAL_FLU 21

  APPEND_PARAMETER COMMAND 16 UINT 0 65535 0 "The scheduled action for the waypoint."
    STATE NAV_WAYPOINT 16
    STATE NAV_LOITER_UNLIM 17

  APPEND_PARAMETER CURRENT 8 UINT 0 255 0 "false:0, true:1"

  APPEND_PARAMETER AUTOCONTINUE 8 UINT 0 255 1 "Autocontinue to next waypoint. 0: false, 1: true. Set false to pause mission after the item completes."

  APPEND_PARAMETER PARAM1 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "PARAM1, see MAV_CMD enum"

  APPEND_PARAMETER PARAM2 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "PARAM2, see MAV_CMD enum"

  APPEND_PARAMETER PARAM3 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "PARAM3, see MAV_CMD enum"

  APPEND_PARAMETER PARAM4 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "PARAM4, see MAV_CMD enum"

  APPEND_PARAMETER X 32 INT MIN MAX 0 "PARAM5 / local: x position in meters * 1e4, global: latitude in degrees * 10^7"

  APPEND_PARAMETER Y 32 INT MIN MAX 0 "PARAM6 / y position: local: x position in meters * 1e4, global: longitude in degrees *10^7"

  APPEND_PARAMETER Z 32 FLOAT MIN MAX 0.0 "PARAM7 / z position: global: altitude in meters (relative or absolute, depending on frame."

  # MAVLink 2 Extension Field
  APPEND_PARAMETER MISSION_TYPE 8 UINT 0 255 0 "Mission type."
    STATE MISSION 0
    STATE FENCE 1
    STATE RALLY 2
    STATE ALL 255
